items.title: Nearly 300 GitHub repos pose as legit software to push malware
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>A threat actor has published hundreds of fake GitHub repositories impersonating legitimate software and security projects to distribute infostealer malware. [...]</article>

items.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update, which includes the July 2026 Patch Tuesday security updates for 570 vulnerabilities, along with additional security fixes. [...]</article>

items.title: Microsoft July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes massive 570 flaws, 3 zero-days
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>Today is Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday, and with it comes security updates for a record-breaking 570 flaws, including two zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in attacks and one publicly disclosed. [...]</article>

items.title: Windows 11 KB5101650 & KB5099414 cumulative updates released
items.author: Mayank Parmar
items.content: <article>Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5101650 and KB5099414 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]</article>

items.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>Progress Software has confirmed that a high-severity zero-day vulnerability is behind the emergency shutdown of ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers last week and has released security updates to patch the flaw. [...]</article>

items.title: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]</article>

items.title: You Don't Have to Run an Exploit to Know If You're Vulnerable
items.author: Sponsored by Picus Software
items.content: <article>Many vulnerabilities cannot be safely validated with live exploits, either because no exploit exists or the affected systems are too critical to test. Picus explains how TTP chaining helps organizations determine exploitability by validating the attack techniques an exploit depends on, without launching the exploit itself. [...]</article>

items.title: Microsoft Entra ID gets passkeys default authentication starting September
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>Microsoft has announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for the Entra ID enterprise identity service starting September 2026. [...]</article>

items.title: New phishing kits target Microsoft 365 accounts, evade MFA
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Two new phishing kits, Jalisco and OmegaLord, have been discovered in attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts, using techniques that defeat multi-factor authentication (MFA). [...]</article>

items.title: SAP warns of critical flaws in NetWeaver and Commerce Cloud
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>SAP has addressed 16 vulnerabilities across multiple products as part of its July 2026 security updates, including three critical flaws in NetWeaver, Commerce Cloud, and AppRouter. [...]</article>

items.title: Microsoft starts testing cleaner Windows Search without ads
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>Microsoft is now testing a cleaner and faster version of Windows Search that should prioritize relevant results over ads and promotional content. [...]</article>

items.title: US sanctions VPN, malware providers for enabling ransomware attacks
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned two individuals and one entity for enabling ransomware attacks against U.S. organizations. [...]</article>

items.title: Japan's largest taxi operator shuts systems after cyberattack
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Japan's largest taxi operator, Nihon Kotsu, announced that its systems were compromised in a cyberattack, forcing the company to shut down part of its infrastructure. [...]</article>

items.title: Hackers backdoor Jscrambler npm package with infostealer malware
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>The Jscrambler client-side web security company disclosed that a threat actor published a malicious version of its npm package that has been downloaded almost 1,500 times. [...]</article>

items.title: New CrashStealer malware poses as Apple crash reporting tool
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>A new macOS information-stealing malware called CrashStealer pretends to be Apple's crash-reporting tool to steal credentials, keychain data, and crypto wallets. [...]</article>
